Why Habitat

Built different.
No equity. No noise.

Most programs pick you. We watch you build. Start with an evening. Go deeper when you're ready.

Test before you commit

Most startup advice skips the hard part: you don't know if an idea is worth six months until you've shown it to real people. Habitat lets you validate in an evening before deciding whether to go deeper.

FAQ

Got questions?

General
What is Habitat?
Habitat runs evening sprints, technical hackathons, and a founder residency across Belgium. You show up with an idea, and you leave with something real — a working prototype, a landing page, your first users. We take no equity, ever. Our programs are free, funded by sponsors who want access to the best builders in Europe.
Do I need to be technical to join?
Not at all. Many of our best MVPs have been built by non-technical founders using AI tools like Cursor, Lovable, and v0, or no-code platforms. The framework works for everyone — what matters is that you want to build something.
Where do events happen?
Currently in four Belgian cities: Leuven, Ghent, Brussels, and Antwerp. We're expanding to more cities soon. All upcoming events are listed on our Luma calendar.
Is there an application process?
For Evenings — no application. You register on Luma and show up. For Hackathons — we select for technical ability and drive. For the Residency — yes, it's selective. You need to have shipped an MVP already. The philosophy is simple: you rise by building, not by applying.
Do you really take no equity?
Zero. Ever. Accelerators typically take 5–7%. We take nothing. Our sponsors fund the events because they want access to deal flow — meaning our incentives are perfectly aligned with yours. You keep everything you build.
How is Habitat different from a hackathon?
Traditional hackathons are 24–48 hours, team-based, and focused on a technical challenge. Habitat Evenings are 3–4 hours, individual-focused, and the output is a business — landing page, prototype, first users — not a demo project. That said, we also run our own Technical Hackathons for deeper builds.
How is this different from Startup Weekend?
Startup Weekend is 54 hours over a full weekend with pitching, team formation, and judges. Habitat Evenings are one evening — no pitching (until the optional 60-second demo at the end), no formal team formation (you build your own thing), and no judges.
Habitat Evenings
What happens at a Habitat Evening?
You arrive with an idea. Over 3–4 hours, you go through a structured sprint: define your problem, build a landing page, create a prototype, and get it in front of real people. At the end, there are optional 60-second lightning demos. Then you go home with something live.
Do I need to come with an idea?
Ideally, yes. The registration asks "What idea are you thinking of building?" If you don't have one, you can join someone's table and contribute to their build. But the best outcomes come from people who arrive with something they want to create.
How much does it cost?
Evenings are free. Food and drinks are provided. Everything is funded by our sponsors.
How many people attend?
Around 40 builders per evening. We cap registration to keep things focused and high-energy. Events tend to sell out within 48 hours, so join the waitlist to get notified early.
Can companies send their teams?
Yes. We also run private Habitat sprints for corporate teams. Contact us for our B2B workshop offering.
Partners & Sponsors
How does sponsorship work?
Sponsors fund our events in exchange for access to deal flow — the builders and startups that come out of our programs. Sponsors cannot present, pitch, or take the stage during events. Habitat remains the primary brand. Get in touch if you're interested.
Can I bring Habitat to my city?
Yes — if you're outside our current cities and want to run a Habitat evening, reach out. We'll walk you through the framework, share the playbook, and help you find a venue. We're actively looking for city leads across Europe.
Can I host Habitat at my venue or coworking space?
Absolutely. We're always looking for great venues. Ideal spaces seat 40+ people at tables (not theater-style), have good Wi-Fi, and allow for food and drinks. Contact us and we'll figure it out together.

[Habitat]
  • Events

Technical Hackathons

One day. One team.
One working product.

Full-day deep builds for developers and technical founders. Investor-backed. Real infrastructure. Real stakes. Ship or learn — either way you win.

Duration 10am – 8pm
Team size 2-3 people
Backed by VC + YC founders
Output Working demo
Register interest → See the format
The format

10 hours of focused building.

No slides. No pitch decks at the start. You arrive, you form a team, you build. At the end, you demo a working product to the investors in the room.

10:00

Kickoff & theme reveal

The technical theme or problem space is announced. Team up, pick your angle, define your scope. You have 30 minutes to align — then you build.

10:30

Building begins

Heads down. Real code, real infrastructure, real product. Mentors from our sponsor network are in the room to unblock you when you're stuck.

13:00

Lunch + mid-day check-in

Break, eat, recalibrate. Optional 5-minute mid-day check-ins with a mentor if you've hit a wall or want to pressure-test your approach.

14:00

Back to building

Afternoon block. Focus on making it work end-to-end, not just look good. A working demo matters more than a polished UI.

18:00

Freeze + prep demos

Code freeze. Two hours to prep your demo — no new features, just make it presentable and tell the story clearly.

19:00

Demo + investor pitches

Each team gets 5 minutes. Live demo, one slide max. Sponsors ask real questions. The best teams get follow-up conversations, not just a prize.

20:00

Awards + networking

Prizes, credits, and introductions. More importantly: the people in this room are who you want to know.

Who attends

Built for people who ship.

This isn't a general hackathon. We select for technical ability and the drive to build real things — not to win competitions.

⚡

Technical founders

You've already shipped something. You build fast. You want a focused day with peers at your level and direct access to investors who get technical products.

🛠

Developers exploring startups

You're a strong engineer who's been thinking about building your own thing. This is a low-stakes way to test if you like the founder experience.

🎓

CS & engineering students

From KU Leuven, UGent, VUB, or elsewhere. You want to build something real, meet people who are doing it, and get in front of investors early.

Backing this

Real investors.
Real deal flow.

Our technical hackathons are backed by investors who want to see what you build — and follow up with the best teams.

Lead sponsor

VC Partner

An active early-stage VC fund backing our first technical hackathons. They're there to find deal flow, mentor builders, and write checks to the right teams.

  • Attend and judge the demo day
  • First look at top teams
  • Cloud credits & API access for participants
Co-sponsor

YC Founder

A YC founder co-sponsoring the event to give back to the technical community — and to find the next generation of builders they want to advise.

  • Office hours during the event
  • Direct intros to their network
  • Prizes for winning teams
What you walk away with

More than a weekend project.

The goal isn't to win a hackathon. The goal is to leave with something worth continuing — and people worth building with.

A working technical demo

Not a mockup. Not a pitch deck. A live product that does something real — and that you built under pressure in 8 hours.

Investor introductions

The sponsors are in the room all day. If your build gets their attention, the conversation starts that evening — not 6 months later.

Your co-founder, maybe

Teams form on the day. Some of the best founder partnerships start at events like this. You won't know until you build together.

FAQ

Got questions?

Technical Hackathons
What are Technical Hackathons?
Full-day deep builds (10am–8pm) for developers and technical founders. You form a team of 2–3, build a working product around a revealed theme, and demo it to investors at the end of the day. Think of it as the next level up from our Evenings.
Do I need a team beforehand?
No. Teams form on the day after the theme is revealed. Some of the best founder partnerships start at events like this. You won't know until you build together.
Who are the sponsors and judges?
Our hackathons are backed by active early-stage VC funds and YC founders. They're in the room all day — mentoring during the build and judging the demos. The best teams get follow-up conversations, not just a prize.
What do I need to bring?
Your laptop, your skills, and the drive to ship something real. We provide the venue, food, mentors, infrastructure, and cloud credits from our sponsors.
When is the first hackathon?
Coming soon. Register your interest and we'll notify you when the first one opens.

Ready to build?

Register your interest and we'll notify you when the next technical hackathon opens.

Register interest → Back to Habitat

[Habitat]

Coming 2026
Habitat Residency

Live and build
with other founders.

You've already shipped an MVP. Now it's time to go deeper. Full immersion, dedicated mentorship, and a demo day in front of investors.

Format Live-in
Ends with Demo day
Join the waitlist → How it works
What it is

From MVP to
business in a week.

Habitat Residency is not a course. Not a coworking space. Not another accelerator. It's one week where you only build.

The premise is simple.

A small cohort of founders, already past idea stage, live and work together for consecutive days. No commute. No context-switching. No meetings except the ones that matter.

You get a dedicated mentor — a practitioner, not a professor — who works alongside you for the duration. You get access to a VC network for feedback and potential funding. And at the end, you pitch.

The goal isn't to raise money immediately (though it happens). The goal is to leave knowing exactly where you're going and why.

5
founders per cohort — small enough to build real trust
7
consecutive days of focused, undistracted building
1
dedicated mentor with you the entire week
0
equity taken — ever
The week

Seven days, structured to ship.

Each day has a clear theme. You're building the whole time — but we make sure you're building the right things in the right order.

Day 1

Arrivals + alignment

Move in, meet your cohort, set your week goal. What does success look like by Friday? Get brutally honest about where you are.

Day 2

Customer conversations

Your first full day is not building. It's talking to users. Structured interviews, real insights, kill your assumptions early.

Day 3 — 4

Deep build

Two full days of heads-down work. Morning standups with your mentor. Afternoon unblocking sessions. Evening reviews with the cohort.

Day 5

Test & iterate

Get real users on your product. Run experiments. Fix what's broken. Stop adding features. Make what you have work better.

Day 6

Story + pitch prep

What's your narrative? Why this, why now, why you? Practice your demo until the story is effortless.

Day 7

Demo Day

Investors, mentors, and the broader Habitat community. 10-minute demo + Q&A. The beginning of the next chapter.

Support structure

Not advice. Presence.

The difference between a good program and a transformative one is whether the people helping you are actually in the room.

Dedicated mentor

One practitioner — a founder, operator, or investor who has done what you're trying to do — works with your cohort for the duration. Not for 2 hours. For the whole time.

VC office hours

Investors from our network drop in for sessions. Not to evaluate you — to help you think clearer. Evaluation happens at Demo Day, not before.

Peer cohort

Other founders at the same stage, with complementary skills, working alongside you. The cohort is small enough to build genuine trust — and honest enough to tell you hard truths.

Who we select

Already past zero.

The Residency is for founders who've proven they can ship — and are ready to figure out whether they can build a business.

✓

You've shipped an MVP

You have a live product — not a landing page, not a deck. Real code, real users, even if early. You know you can build.

✓

You're pre or early revenue

You have some validation but you're not yet at scale. The Residency accelerates the period between "it works" and "it grows."

✓

You can be all-in for the duration

No day job meetings. No half-presence. Complete full focus. If you can't commit to that, the timing isn't right yet.

✓

You want to be challenged

The cohort and mentor will push you. Good feedback is uncomfortable. You need to want that, not just tolerate it.

Not for everyone

Be honest with yourself.

The Residency will make great founders better. It won't turn someone who isn't ready into a founder.

✕
You're still validating whether you want to be a founder
✕
You haven't built or shipped anything yet
✕
You're looking for co-founders (try our evenings first)
✕
You can't step away from your current obligations for this intensive period
The culmination

Demo Day.

Day seven isn't a formality. It's the moment your week of work meets the people who can help you go further.

Demo Day is a curated event open to investors from our network, the broader Habitat community, and select guests. Each founder presents a live product demo, not slides.

Q&A follows. The conversation continues at the networking session after. Some founders leave with term sheets. Most leave with introductions, feedback, and a clearer path.

All of them leave knowing exactly what the next 90 days look like.

Live product demos only

No decks. No mockups. Your product, live, in front of people who matter.

Curated investor audience

Early-stage VCs and angels from our network — people who write checks at this stage.

Habitat community

Alumni from our evenings and hackathons. Your first potential early adopters are in the room.

Post-event introductions

We personally make intros from Demo Day onwards. The event is a start, not a finish.

FAQ

Got questions?

Habitat Residency
What is the Residency?
A small cohort of founders, full immersion. You live and build together with a dedicated mentor. The end of the residency is Demo Day in front of investors. It's not an accelerator — there's no curriculum, no lectures. Just focused building, honest feedback, and a push to go from MVP to business.
Who is the Residency for?
Founders who have already shipped an MVP — real code, real users, even if early. You should be pre- or early-revenue, able to commit to a full week with zero distractions, and genuinely want to be challenged by peers and mentors.
How do I get in?
The Residency is selective. The best performers from our Evenings get invited to Hackathons, and the best hackathon teams get first look at Residency spots. Merit shows up in code, not cover letters. Applications open in 2026 — join the waitlist.
Does the Residency cost anything?
Details on costs and logistics will be shared when applications open. What we can confirm: we take zero equity. That will never change.
What happens on Demo Day?
Each founder presents a live product demo (no slides), followed by Q&A from a curated audience of early-stage VCs, angels, and the Habitat community. Some founders leave with term sheets. Most leave with introductions, feedback, and a clear path forward.

Applications opening soon.

Join the waitlist and we'll reach out when the first cohort opens. Cohort size is small. We're selective.

Join the waitlist → Back to Habitat

Why Habitat

Built different.
No equity. No noise.

Most programs pick you. We watch you build. Start with an evening. Go deeper when you're ready.

Test before you commit

Most startup advice skips the hard part: you don't know if an idea is worth six months until you've shown it to real people. Habitat lets you validate in an evening before deciding whether to go deeper.

You rise by building, not applying

We don't select founders from applications. The best people in our evenings get invited to hackathons. The best hackathon teams get first look at Residency spots. Merit shows up in code, not cover letters.

Accelerators take 7%. We take nothing. Our sponsors fund these events because they want deal flow — which means our incentives are perfectly aligned with yours.

Stories

From the people who shipped.

"Was building alone in Leuven. Week 5 I had a team and users."

Leon Dehullu
Founder, Stealth startup

"Came for the vibe. Stayed because I actually launched and found people who ship."

Vincent Demarez
Founder, Friday
FAQ

Got questions?

General
What is Habitat?
Habitat runs evening sprints, technical hackathons, and a founder residency across Belgium. You show up with an idea, and you leave with something real — a working prototype, a landing page, your first users. We take no equity, ever. Our programs are free, funded by sponsors who want access to the best builders in Europe.
Do I need to be technical to join?
Not at all. Many of our best MVPs have been built by non-technical founders using AI tools like Cursor, Lovable, and v0, or no-code platforms. The framework works for everyone — what matters is that you want to build something.
Where do events happen?
Currently in four Belgian cities: Leuven, Ghent, Brussels, and Antwerp. We're expanding to more cities soon. All upcoming events are listed on our Luma calendar.
Is there an application process?
For Evenings — no application. You register on Luma and show up. For Hackathons — we select for technical ability and drive. For the Residency — yes, it's selective. You need to have shipped an MVP already. The philosophy is simple: you rise by building, not by applying.
Do you really take no equity?
Zero. Ever. Accelerators typically take 5–7%. We take nothing. Our sponsors fund the events because they want access to deal flow — meaning our incentives are perfectly aligned with yours. You keep everything you build.
How is Habitat different from a hackathon?
Traditional hackathons are 24–48 hours, team-based, and focused on a technical challenge. Habitat Evenings are 3–4 hours, individual-focused, and the output is a business — landing page, prototype, first users — not a demo project. That said, we also run our own Technical Hackathons for deeper builds.
How is this different from Startup Weekend?
Startup Weekend is 54 hours over a full weekend with pitching, team formation, and judges. Habitat Evenings are one evening — no pitching (until the optional 60-second demo at the end), no formal team formation (you build your own thing), and no judges.
Habitat Evenings
What happens at a Habitat Evening?
You arrive with an idea. Over 3–4 hours, you go through a structured sprint: define your problem, build a landing page, create a prototype, and get it in front of real people. At the end, there are optional 60-second lightning demos. Then you go home with something live.
Do I need to come with an idea?
Ideally, yes. The registration asks "What idea are you thinking of building?" If you don't have one, you can join someone's table and contribute to their build. But the best outcomes come from people who arrive with something they want to create.
How much does it cost?
Evenings are free. Food and drinks are provided. Everything is funded by our sponsors.
How many people attend?
Around 40 builders per evening. We cap registration to keep things focused and high-energy. Events tend to sell out within 48 hours, so join the waitlist to get notified early.
Can companies send their teams?
Yes. We also run private Habitat sprints for corporate teams. Contact us for our B2B workshop offering.
Partners & Sponsors
How does sponsorship work?
Sponsors fund our events in exchange for access to deal flow — the builders and startups that come out of our programs. Sponsors cannot present, pitch, or take the stage during events. Habitat remains the primary brand. Get in touch if you're interested.
Can I bring Habitat to my city?
Yes — if you're outside our current cities and want to run a Habitat evening, reach out. We'll walk you through the framework, share the playbook, and help you find a venue. We're actively looking for city leads across Europe.
Can I host Habitat at my venue or coworking space?
Absolutely. We're always looking for great venues. Ideal spaces seat 40+ people at tables (not theater-style), have good Wi-Fi, and allow for food and drinks. Contact us and we'll figure it out together.

[Habitat]
  • Events
  • Hackathons
  • Residency
  • Why Habitat
  • Join waitlist →
Technical Hackathons

One day. One team.
One working product.

Full-day deep builds for developers and technical founders. Investor-backed. Real infrastructure. Real stakes. Ship or learn — either way you win.

Duration 10am – 8pm
Team size 2-3 people
Backed by VC + YC founders
Output Working demo
Register interest → See the format
The format

10 hours of focused building.

No slides. No pitch decks at the start. You arrive, you form a team, you build. At the end, you demo a working product to the investors in the room.

10:00

Kickoff & theme reveal

The technical theme or problem space is announced. Team up, pick your angle, define your scope. You have 30 minutes to align — then you build.

10:30

Building begins

Heads down. Real code, real infrastructure, real product. Mentors from our sponsor network are in the room to unblock you when you're stuck.

13:00

Lunch + mid-day check-in

Break, eat, recalibrate. Optional 5-minute mid-day check-ins with a mentor if you've hit a wall or want to pressure-test your approach.

14:00

Back to building

Afternoon block. Focus on making it work end-to-end, not just look good. A working demo matters more than a polished UI.

18:00

Freeze + prep demos

Code freeze. Two hours to prep your demo — no new features, just make it presentable and tell the story clearly.

19:00

Demo + investor pitches

Each team gets 5 minutes. Live demo, one slide max. Sponsors ask real questions. The best teams get follow-up conversations, not just a prize.

20:00

Awards + networking

Prizes, credits, and introductions. More importantly: the people in this room are who you want to know.

Who attends

Built for people who ship.

This isn't a general hackathon. We select for technical ability and the drive to build real things — not to win competitions.

⚡

Technical founders

You've already shipped something. You build fast. You want a focused day with peers at your level and direct access to investors who get technical products.

🛠

Developers exploring startups

You're a strong engineer who's been thinking about building your own thing. This is a low-stakes way to test if you like the founder experience.

🎓

CS & engineering students

From KU Leuven, UGent, VUB, or elsewhere. You want to build something real, meet people who are doing it, and get in front of investors early.

Backing this

Real investors.
Real deal flow.

Our technical hackathons are backed by investors who want to see what you build — and follow up with the best teams.

Lead sponsor

VC Partner

An active early-stage VC fund backing our first technical hackathons. They're there to find deal flow, mentor builders, and write checks to the right teams.

  • Attend and judge the demo day
  • First look at top teams
  • Cloud credits & API access for participants
Co-sponsor

YC Founder

A YC founder co-sponsoring the event to give back to the technical community — and to find the next generation of builders they want to advise.

  • Office hours during the event
  • Direct intros to their network
  • Prizes for winning teams
What you walk away with

More than a weekend project.

The goal isn't to win a hackathon. The goal is to leave with something worth continuing — and people worth building with.

A working technical demo

Not a mockup. Not a pitch deck. A live product that does something real — and that you built under pressure in 8 hours.

Investor introductions

The sponsors are in the room all day. If your build gets their attention, the conversation starts that evening — not 6 months later.

Your co-founder, maybe

Teams form on the day. Some of the best founder partnerships start at events like this. You won't know until you build together.

FAQ

Got questions?

Technical Hackathons
What are Technical Hackathons?
Full-day deep builds (10am–8pm) for developers and technical founders. You form a team of 2–3, build a working product around a revealed theme, and demo it to investors at the end of the day. Think of it as the next level up from our Evenings.
Do I need a team beforehand?
No. Teams form on the day after the theme is revealed. Some of the best founder partnerships start at events like this. You won't know until you build together.
Who are the sponsors and judges?
Our hackathons are backed by active early-stage VC funds and YC founders. They're in the room all day — mentoring during the build and judging the demos. The best teams get follow-up conversations, not just a prize.
What do I need to bring?
Your laptop, your skills, and the drive to ship something real. We provide the venue, food, mentors, infrastructure, and cloud credits from our sponsors.
When is the first hackathon?
Coming soon. Register your interest and we'll notify you when the first one opens.

Ready to build?

Register your interest and we'll notify you when the next technical hackathon opens.

Register interest → Back to Habitat
